title: Quick-Fix record (one append-only line)
description: One entry in the append-only Quick-Fix record log (.orchestrator/quickfix/records.jsonl — JSON Lines, one object per line). This is an APPEND-ONLY PROTOCOL for observability/audit, NOT a tamper-proof ledger (no hash chain). A record MUST NOT be read as an activation credential for any later session — activation is always a fresh human launcher invocation (process/quickfix-lane.md). Normative source: process/quickfix-lane.md.

outcome.description: completed = passed preliminary guard -> verification -> final guard and produced a result commit; escalated = stopped and handed off (investigation preserved before teardown).

baseline_sha.description: The worktree HEAD SHA captured at launch — the baseline the guard bound to.

ts.description: Injected timestamp (the launcher provides it; the core never reads the clock itself).

branch.description: completed: the quickfix/<request_id> branch carrying the result commit (must start with 'quickfix/' — the result is never on a shared branch). Never auto-applied to the adopter's current branch; the human decides whether to cherry-pick.

stat.description: completed: `git show --stat` summary of the result commit.

verification.description: completed: the targeted verification that ran between the preliminary and final guard.

escalation_reason.description: escalated: why the lane stopped (scope expansion / protected surface hit / verification failure / unprovable eligibility / uncertainty).

patch_hash.description: escalated: hash of the preserved patch (so the saved investigation is identifiable).

patch_path.description: escalated: where the patch was saved BEFORE worktree teardown (outside the ephemeral worktree).

diff_summary.description: escalated: short stat/diff summary of the preserved work.

handoff_path.description: escalated: the escalation handoff doc instructing the human to relaunch Full.
